tools.title: nmap
tools.purpose: Network scanner: finds live hosts, open ports, service versions and OS, runs NSE scripts. The first recon step on almost any machine.

flags.desc: Ping scan only (host discovery) without port scanning - find live hosts in a subnet.
Skip host discovery, treat the target as live. Needed when the host blocks ICMP (typical on HTB).
List Scan: only list and resolve targets without sending packets.
Skip reverse DNS resolution (speeds up the scan).
Always do DNS resolution, even for unreachable hosts.
TCP SYN ping on the given ports for host discovery.
TCP ACK ping on the given ports.
Use your own DNS servers for resolution.
TCP SYN (half-open / stealth) scan. Default when running as root, fast and less noticeable.
TCP Connect scan (full connection). Default without root privileges.
UDP scan. Slow - usually combined with --top-ports.
TCP ACK scan - maps firewall rules (filtered/unfiltered).
TCP Null scan (no flags) - bypasses simple filters.
TCP FIN scan - bypasses simple filters.
TCP Xmas scan (FIN+PSH+URG).
IP protocol scan (which protocols the host supports).
Scan only the given ports. Ranges (1-1000) and T:/U: prefixes are supported.
Scan all 65535 ports.
Scan the N most common ports.
Fast mode - only the top 100 ports.
Exclude the given ports from the scan.
Scan ports in order, do not randomize.
Service version detection on open ports.
Version scan intensity: 0 (light) ... 9 (all probes).
Try all version detection probes (= intensity 9).
Operating system detection by network fingerprints.
Guess the OS more aggressively when there is no exact match.
Run the set of default NSE scripts (equivalent to --script=default).
Run NSE scripts by name/category/folder (default, safe, vuln, auth, brute, discovery).
Pass arguments to NSE scripts (n1=v1,n2=v2).
Aggressive mode: enables -O, -sV, -sC and --traceroute all at once.
Timing template 0..5 (paranoid..insane). T3 is the default, T4 is fast for labs/CTF.
No slower than N packets per second - speeds up a full port scan.
No faster than N packets per second - gentle mode for WAF/IDS.
Limit on probe retransmissions per port.
Drop a host if it is not fully scanned within this time (ms/s/m/h suffixes).
Minimum delay between probes to a host (rate-limit bypass).
Fragment packets into small IP fragments (-ff is stronger).
Fragment with the given MTU (multiple of 8).
Mask the scan with decoys (decoy1,decoy2,ME,...).
Spoof the source IP.
Use the given source port (bypass source-port filters).
Spoof the MAC (0 = random, or a prefix/vendor).
Use the given network interface.
Save all three formats at once: scan.nmap, scan.xml, scan.gnmap.
Save normal (human-readable) output to a file.
Save output as XML.
Save grepable output (handy for grep/awk).
Verbose output (-vv is even more verbose).
Show the reason a port is in its given state.
Show only open (and open|filtered) ports.
Show all sent/received packets (debugging).
Take targets from a file (one per line).
Pick N random targets (0 = infinite).
Exclude the given hosts/networks from the scan.
Enable IPv6 scanning.
Resume an interrupted scan from normal/grepable output.

recipes.note: Standard first scan: default scripts + versions, save all formats.
Fast full sweep of all TCP ports; then do a targeted rescan of what was found.
Deep scan only of the open ports found during the sweep.
UDP scan of the top 100 ports (UDP is slow - keep the volume limited).
Run NSE scripts from the vuln category for known vulnerabilities.
Stealth SYN with host-discovery skipped, when ICMP is blocked (HTB).

tools.notes: The target is positional (no flag), accepts a hostname/IP/CIDR (10.0.0.0/24) and ranges (10.0.0.1-50). -sS and -O require root. By default the top 1000 ports are scanned. -sn is the modern name for the old -sP.

tools.title: ffuf
tools.purpose: Fast Go-based web fuzzer: substitutes words from a wordlist at the FUZZ keyword in the URL, header, body or cookies. Directory/file brute-forcing, parameters, vhosts, value enumeration.

flags.desc: Wordlist; you can bind a keyword: wordlist.txt:KEY. Repeatable for multiple wordlists.
Multi-wordlist mode: clusterbomb (all combinations), pitchfork (by index), sniper (one position at a time).
List of extensions appended to the FUZZ keyword (comma-separated).
Raw HTTP request from a file (e.g. saved from Burp) with FUZZ inside.
Protocol for the raw request (http/https).
Ignore comments in the wordlist (lines starting with #).
dirsearch wordlist compatibility mode (used with -e).
Target URL. The FUZZ keyword goes where you want to iterate.
HTTP request method.
Header. Repeatable. For vhost fuzzing: -H 'Host: FUZZ.target'.
POST request body (can contain FUZZ).
Cookies in the format 'n1=v1; n2=v2'.
Follow redirects.
Proxy for all requests (HTTP/SOCKS5).
Replay only matched requests through a proxy (into Burp).
Recursive scan (the URL must end with FUZZ).
Maximum recursion depth.
HTTP request timeout in seconds.
Use HTTP/2.
Show only these response codes (or all). Default: 200-299,301,302,307,401,403,405,500.
Match by response size (bytes).
Match by the number of lines in the response.
Match by the number of words in the response.
Match by a regexp in the response.
Match by time to first byte (ms).
Matcher logic: and / or (default or).
Hide these response codes (comma-separated, ranges supported).
Hide by response size (bytes) - the classic against noisy 404s.
Hide by the number of lines.
Hide by the number of words.
Hide by a regexp match.
Hide by time to first byte.
Filter logic: and / or (default or).
Auto-calibrate filters (figures out the noisy baseline on its own).
Number of threads (default 40).
Requests-per-second limit (0 = no limit).
Delay between requests: fixed '0.1' or a range '0.1-2.0'.
Maximum runtime of the whole process (sec).
Silent mode - results only, no banner or progress.
Colored output.
Verbose output - full URL and redirect location.
Stop if >95% of responses are 403 Forbidden.
Write results to a file.
File format: json, ejson, html, md, csv, ecsv, all.
Folder to save the bodies of matched responses.
Do not create an output file if there are no results.

recipes.note: Directory/file brute-forcing: show everything and filter out noise by size.
File brute-forcing with extensions and filter auto-calibration.
Virtual host enumeration; filter the size of the default response.
Recursive brute-forcing with an appended extension (URL ends with FUZZ).
Fuzzing a raw request from Burp with FUZZ inside the file.

tools.notes: The FUZZ keyword is substituted into any part of the request. For multiple wordlists use -w file:KEY and -mode. The default code matcher is 200-299,301,302,307,401,403,405,500. Typical workflow: -mc all + a filter by size/words/lines.

tools.title: feroxbuster
tools.purpose: Fast recursive content scanner in Rust. Brute-forces directories and files, by default extracts links from responses and automatically recurses. Convenient auto modes (--smart/--thorough).

flags.desc: Target URL (short/long: -u/--url). Repeatable.
Wordlist path or URL (-w/--wordlist).
Read a list of URLs from STDIN.
Resume an unfinished scan from a state file.
Raw HTTP request as a template for all requests.
Proxy to http://127.0.0.1:8080 + disable TLS verification (one command for Burp).
Send only unfiltered (found) requests to Burp.
Enables --auto-tune, --collect-words and --collect-backups.
Like --smart, plus --collect-extensions and --scan-dir-listings (the maximum-effort mode).
POST with Content-Type form-urlencoded and this body (@file supported).
Extensions to search for (-x/--extensions), comma- or space-separated, without the dot. @file also works.
HTTP headers (-H/--headers). Repeatable.
Cookies (-b/--cookies).
URL query parameters (-Q/--query).
HTTP method(s) (-m/--methods), default GET.
Request body (@file supported to read from a file).
User-Agent (-a/--user-agent).
Random User-Agent (-A/--random-agent).
Append a / to every URL (-f/--add-slash).
Status code whitelist (-s/--status-codes): show only these. Default - all.
Status code blacklist (-C/--filter-status): hide these.
Hide by response size (-S/--filter-size).
Hide by the number of words (-W/--filter-words).
Hide by the number of lines (-N/--filter-lines).
Hide by a regexp in the body/headers (-X/--filter-regex).
Hide pages similar to the given one (fighting soft-404).
Do not auto-filter wildcard responses (-D/--dont-filter).
Maximum recursion depth (-d/--depth); 0 = infinite, default 4.
Do not scan recursively (-n/--no-recursion).
Force recursion into all found endpoints.
Extract links from the response body (-e/--extract-links); ON by default.
Do not extract links from responses.
Number of threads (-t/--threads), default 50.
Limit on concurrent scans (-L/--scan-limit), 0 = no limit.
Requests-per-second limit per directory.
URLs/regexps excluded from recursion and scanning.
Automatically slow down on a spike in errors.
Automatically stop the scan on a spike in errors.
Force recursion into directory listings.
Automatically discover extensions and add them to --extensions (-E/--collect-extensions).
Automatically request backup extensions for found URLs (-B/--collect-backups): ~ .bak .old .1 ...
Extract meaningful words from responses and add them to the wordlist (-g/--collect-words).
Extensions ignored during auto-collection (-I/--dont-collect).
Disable TLS certificate verification (-k/--insecure). Needed for self-signed on HTB.
Follow redirects (-r/--redirects).
Request timeout in seconds (-T/--timeout), default 7.
Proxy (-p/--proxy): http(s):// or socks5://.
Replay proxy (-P/--replay-proxy): send only the found requests there.
Output file (-o/--output); with --json - JSON records.
Write logs/output as JSON.
URLs only and no logs (for piping into other tools).
Hide progress bars and the banner (-q/--quiet).
Increase verbosity (-v/--verbosity, repeatable).

recipes.note: Basic recursive brute-forcing with extensions.
HTB: ignore self-signed TLS, follow redirects, hide 404/403.
Maximum-effort mode: auto-collect extensions/words/backups + listings.
All traffic through Burp on 127.0.0.1:8080 + ignore TLS.
Screen out soft-404 by similarity to a known nonexistent page.

tools.notes: Link extraction (-e) is enabled by default. -d is the recursion depth, and --data has NO short form (a common confusion). The default wordlist is raft-medium-directories from SecLists. -s is the code whitelist, -C is the blacklist. Scan state is saved to .state for --resume-from.

tools.title: gobuster
tools.purpose: Multi-mode Go brute-forcer: directories/files (dir), DNS subdomains (dns), virtual hosts (vhost) and arbitrary fuzzing on the FUZZ keyword (fuzz). Fast and simple.

modes.name: dir
modes.desc: Brute-force directories and files by URL.

modes.name: dns
modes.desc: Brute-force a domain's subdomains via DNS.

modes.name: fuzz
modes.desc: Fuzz on the FUZZ keyword in the URL/header/body.

flags.desc: Path to the wordlist (- = STDIN). Required.
Number of threads (default 10).
File to write results to (defaults to stdout).
Delay between requests per thread.
Do not print the banner or extra noise (-q/--quiet).
Do not show progress (-np/--no-progress).
Do not show errors (-ne/--no-error).
File with replacement patterns (-p/--pattern).
Enable debug output.
Target URL (-u/--url). dir/vhost/fuzz modes.
Cookies for requests (-c/--cookies).
HTTP headers (-H/--headers). Repeatable.
Follow redirects (-r/--follow-redirect).
Skip TLS certificate verification (-k/--no-tls-validation). Needed for self-signed.
User-Agent (-a/--useragent).
Random User-Agent (-rua/--random-agent).
Proxy: http(s):// or socks5://.
HTTP timeout (-to/--timeout), default 10s.
HTTP method (-m/--method), default GET.
Username for Basic Auth (-U/--username).
Password for Basic Auth (-P/--password).
File extensions, comma-separated, without the dot.
Status code blacklist (--status-codes-blacklist), default 404. Overrides -s.
Status code whitelist. To enable it, first clear the blacklist: -b ''.
Read the extension list from a file (-X/--extensions-file).
Expanded mode - print full URLs (-e/--expanded).
Do not print status codes (-n/--no-status).
Append a / to every request (-f/--add-slash).
On a file hit, look for backup copies (-db/--discover-backup).
Exclude responses by body length (-xl/--exclude-length), ranges allowed.
Hide the body length in the output (-hl/--hide-length).
Target domain (-do/--domain). In dns mode the target is given as a domain.
Also check CNAME records (-c/--check-cname).
Keep going when a wildcard is detected (-wc/--wildcard).
Custom DNS server (server or server:port); on HTB this is usually the target host itself. Does not work on Windows.
DNS resolver timeout (default 1s).
Append the base domain to every wordlist entry (-ad/--append-domain). Without it the words are used as-is.
Exclude these status codes (-xs/--exclude-status).
Exclude responses by body length (-xl/--exclude-length).
Auto-tune exclude-length to the hostname length in responses (-xh/--exclude-hostname-length).
Excluded status codes (-b/--exclude-statuscodes). The FUZZ keyword is required in the URL/-H/-B.
Exclude responses by body length (-xl/--exclude-length).
Request body (may contain the FUZZ keyword) (-B/--body).

recipes.note: Brute-force directories and files with extensions.
Switch to a status code whitelist (clear the blacklist first).
Brute-force subdomains via DNS; on HTB add --resolver pointing at the target host for *.htb.
Brute-force virtual hosts; without --append-domain nothing will be found.
Arbitrary fuzzing where dir does not fit (header/body/path).

tools.notes: v3.8.x uses urfave/cli: short aliases can be multi-letter (-to, -np, -ad, -xl) and are called with a single dash. In dir the default is blacklist 404; you cannot use -s and -b at the same time. In vhost on v3.2+ the domain is NOT appended without --append-domain - a common cause of 'empty' results. In dns mode there is no -u and no HTTP flags.

tools.title: dirsearch
tools.purpose: Flexible Python web path scanner. Brute-forces directories and files, supports the %EXT% placeholder for extensions, recursion, rich filtering by code/size/text and output in various formats.

flags.desc: Target URL (-u/--url). Repeatable.
File with a list of URLs (-l/--urls-file).
Read URLs from STDIN.
Target is a subnet in CIDR format (e.g. 10.0.0.0/24).
Load a raw HTTP request from a file (requires --scheme).
Session file for resuming (-s/--session).
Wordlists or folders, comma-separated (-w/--wordlists). Default - db/dicc.txt.
Comma-separated list of extensions (-e/--extensions). Fills in %EXT% in the wordlist.
Append extensions and a / to every word (-f/--force-extensions).
Replace the words' existing extension with yours.
Exclude these extensions.
Prepend prefixes to all words.
Append suffixes to all words.
Convert the wordlist to uppercase (-U/--uppercase).
Convert the wordlist to lowercase (-L/--lowercase).
Show only these codes (-i/--include-status). Ranges allowed.
Hide these codes (-x/--exclude-status).
Hide responses by size (comma-separated).
Hide responses containing this text. Repeatable.
Hide responses by a regexp.
Recursive brute-forcing (-r/--recursive).
Maximum recursion depth (-R/--max-recursion-depth).
Codes that trigger recursion (default 200-399,401,403).
Scan the given subdirectories of the target.
Minimum response length to show.
Maximum response length to show.
HTTP method (-m/--http-method), default GET.
Request body (-d/--data).
HTTP header (-H/--header). Repeatable.
Follow redirects (-F/--follow-redirects).
Random User-Agent on each request.
Credentials (e.g. user:pass or a bearer token).
Authentication type: basic, digest, bearer, ntlm.
Cookie header.
Fixed User-Agent.
Number of threads (-t/--threads), default 25.
Connection timeout (default 7.5).
Delay between requests.
Proxy (HTTP/SOCKS), repeatable (-p/--proxy).
Proxy credentials.
Maximum requests per second.
Number of retries for failed requests.
Scheme for a raw request or a URL without a scheme.
Crawl found responses looking for new paths.
Show full URLs in the output.
Output file (-o/--output-file).
Report formats, comma-separated (-O/--output-formats): simple, plain, json, xml, md, csv, html, sqlite.
Quiet mode (-q/--quiet-mode).
Verbose output (-v/--verbose): response time and content-type.
No colored output.

recipes.note: Basic scan with extensions (substituted into %EXT% in the wordlist).
Recursion to depth 3, hide 404/403.
Screen out empty responses, random UA, follow redirects.
Run through Burp to analyze requests.
Scan saving the report as JSON for further processing.

tools.notes: How %EXT% works: dirsearch replaces the %EXT% keyword in wordlist entries with the extensions from -e (index.%EXT% + -e asp,aspx -> index, index.asp, index.aspx). The default wordlist db/dicc.txt already uses %EXT%. TLS certificates are not verified by default - there is no separate -k/--insecure. In recent versions the flags were renamed: output is now -o/--output-file, formats -O/--output-formats.
